items.title: Guinea bans export of raw gold in bid to boost revenues
items.description: Guinea outlaws the export of raw gold in a bid to increase domestic processing of the valuable natural resource.

items.title: West Asia war updates: Vance says talks with Iranian officials set ’good foundation’ for a deal to end the war
items.description: Mr. Vance and U.S. officials claimed progress on multiple fronts, including the establishment of “mechanisms” to ensure the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for global energy shipments, stays open

items.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
items.description: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

items.title: Messi family seeks privacy with statement on health of player’s father
items.description: Jorge Messi, Argentina skipper’s father, is recovering from 'health issue,’ confirms family in a statement; Lionel broke down in tears during match against Algeria and hinted afterwards it was a family issue. Leer más

items.title: Political crisis deepens as Romanian lawmakers reject new government
items.description: The failed confidence vote against Mr. Vestea’s proposed cabinet is expected to deepen a period of instability and turmoil, as the country currently faces one of the largest budget deficits in the European Union

items.title: 'Ridiculous and disrespectful': Inquiry into NDIS cuts extended
items.description: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

items.title: Students protest AI amid growing national concerns
items.description: As Australia tracks toward becoming a global leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, a group of high school students is demanding stronger regulation.

items.title: England: Lawn Mower Championships: 'Proper British fun'
items.description: Dozens of speed demons are competing at the British Lawn Mower Championships in Chichester, southern England.These mowers have been modified and have had their blades removed for optimal speed. They can reach an impressive 55mph (88kmph) at top speed. While often seen as a fun motorsport, the racing is incredibly physical.

items.title: Orion CMC in advanced discussions for three Asian partnerships
EU approves €76 million German state aid for cutting edge semiconductor facility
World Cup 2026: Haaland strikes twice as Norway secure knockout progression
Asake Partners Spotify for One-Night-Only Performance of M$NEY in London
items.description: The exclusive one-night performance of his latest album M$NEY was held at London’s Theatre Royal Drury Lane in partnership with Spotify. The post Asake Partners Spotify for One-Night-Only Performance of M$NEY in London appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: US: Trump threatens prison for Reflecting Pool 'vandalism'
items.description: The Trump administration spent $14.7 million repainting the pool "American flag blue" ahead of US' 250th anniversary celebrations. Weeks later, the paint is peeling off and algae has turned the water green.

items.title: Iran set to return to mainstream oil market as US temporarily waives sanctions
items.description: As 60 days of peace negotiations between the US and Iran continue, Tehran appears to have won significant concessions from Washington. Chief among those is the waiving of sanctions on Iranian oil exports. US sanctions have been in place for decades, cutting Iran off from mainstream global oil markets and forcing it to sell its oil to China at discount rates. The US says the other side of the deal is that Iran will allow international inspectors access to its nuclear program - Tehran though says it has made no such commitment.

items.title: US gov’t investigates New York coffee chain over ban on pro-Israel lawmaker
items.description: Top civil rights prosecutor announces probe after cafe chain says it will not serve Democratic lawmaker Dan Goldman.

items.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
items.description: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

items.title: Two dead in car crash in Tasmania's south
items.description: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

items.title: World Cup 2026: France into last 32 with win over Iraq despite two-hour weather delay
items.description: With a second win in two matches, Les Bleus secured their place in the World Cup round of 32. Although they were never really troubled by Iraq (3-0), France were mainly disrupted by a storm over Philadelphia, which suspended the match for almost two hours between the first and second halves.

items.description: 'Cuadernos' graft trial hears from pilots, who allege presidential planes were used for delivery of suitcases and materials. Leer más

items.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
items.description: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

items.title: Dogmatism, clumsiness force Milei off a winning wave
items.description: What can stop Milei? The answer is very simple: his programmatic extremism and political clumsiness. Leer más

items.title: Argentina confirms awarding of concession for strategic Paraná-Paraguay waterway
items.description: Government confirms consortium of Belgian company Jan De Nul and local firm Servimagnus will be granted a 25-year concession to dredge and maintain Paraguay-Paraná waterway. Leer más

items.title: Belgium issues visas to Taliban delegation for EU meeting
items.description: The meeting is expected to focus on ways to deport some Afghan migrants

items.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
items.description: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

items.title: Spreading violence and impunity spreading perceptions of genocide in Nigeria, UN says
items.description: Spreading violence and impunity for perpetrators is undermining religious freedom in Nigeria, a United Nations Special Rapporteur told a press conference in Abuja on Friday.

items.title: Oil 'pouring through' Strait of Hormuz, Trump says, as he unveils new Air Force One
items.description: US president Donald Trump unveiled the new Airforce One, a jumbo jet formerly owned by Qatar. It features a navy blue and red exterior, a break from the traditional robin’s egg blue.

items.title: Lebanon war damage estimated at $1.3 billion, but who will foot the bill?
items.description: Massive destruction in southern Lebanon in the latest Israel-Hezbollah war is estimated at around $1.38 billion, according to a UN agency and a Lebanese research centre. Reporting from Beirut, FRANCE 24’s Renée Davis notes that while the US-Iran deal includes a proposed $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran, Lebanon’s reconstruction was not mentioned in the 14-point agreement.

items.title: Maradona was 'shielded' by people around him before death, trial hears
items.description: Witness echoed testimony from previous witnesses, including relatives, doctors and healthcare workers, who have described the house where Maradona was looked after as dirty and lacking the necessary medical equipment. Leer más

items.title: Arab League confirms Egyptian diplomat Nabil Fahmy as secretary general
items.description: The Arab League on Monday confirmed Egyptian veteran diplomat Nabil Fahmy as its new chief during a meeting of foreign ministers in Amman, a statement said.

items.title: Milei shows support for under-fire Adorni at Flag Day rally
items.description: President stands by close ally Manuel Adorni despite mounting pressure over undeclared assets and growing calls for his resignation. Leer más

items.title: Alan Greenspan, former US Federal Reserve chairman, dies at 100
items.description: Alan Greenspan, one of the most influential economic policymakers in modern US history, has died aged 100.

items.title: Iran's president heads to Pakistan as U.S.-Iran teams work on war-ending deal
items.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian's visit to Islamabad comes as technical teams were working on details of the deal, following high-level negotiations in Switzerland on Monday led by US Vice President JD Vance and Iran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf.

items.title: Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen, dies aged 94
items.description: The record company executive also worked with Billy Joel, Pink Floyd, Alicia Keys and Aerosmith.

items.title: SA pushes for AI data centres as industry says it's not asking for subsidies
items.description: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

items.title: France faces historically hot temperatures as heatwave carries on
items.description: Millions of people across France woke up drenched in sweat on June 23 after another night of scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme and exceptional temperatures. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from the Eiffel Tower in Paris.

items.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
items.description: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

items.title: Iran war day 116: US eases Iran sanctions; Lebanon ceasefire holds
items.description: US eases oil sanctions for 60 days after Iran agrees to allow international nuclear inspections.

items.title: Manuel Adorni asset declaration shows wealth grew 45-fold in 2024
items.description: Cabinet chief’s documentation attempts to explain an astronomical rise in his net worth. His legal strategy and mounting complications in Congress. Leer más
